video:video.video:title: Semenya criticizes IOC chief Coventry after policy shift on women's Olympic events
video:video.video:description: Two-time Olympic champion runner Caster Semenya on Sunday expressed her disappointment with International Olympic Committee President Kirsty Coventry over the decision to ban transgender athletes from competing in women's events at the Olympics.

video:video.video:title: Kosovo, one of Europe's poorest countries, struggles as Iran war drives up fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Kushtrim Ajvazi is proud to be part of a successful business in one of the poorest countries in Europe.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 30
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday March 30th: Trump says U.S. and Iran negotiating directly and indirectly; Iran strikes water and electrical plant in Kuwait; U.S. airports urge passengers to arrive early due to long lines; Mission to moon set to blast off this week.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong and Asian markets down as oil price worries dominate
video:video.video:description: Stock markets in Asia slumped in Monday morning trading, as worries continued about soaring oil prices and the risk of US escalation in the war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: White House says the Iranian officials ‘are appearing more reasonable behind the scenes’ in talks
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt says Iranian officials are appearing "more reasonable" than former regime leaders, "who are now no longer on planet Earth."

video:video.video:title: Leavitt insists US military will act within law as Trump threatens Iran's energy sites
video:video.video:description: The White House said Monday talks with Iran are going well, but warned President Donald Trump is "not afraid" to carry out threats against Iranian energy sites if they fail.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 30
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 30th: White House insists no change in Cuba sanction policy; Israel's parliament approves death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis; NASA prepares for moon rocket launch; Peruvian activist and her piglet bring attention to animal rights

video:video.video:title: MLB introduces new replay hand signal: two pats on the head
video:video.video:description: Major League Baseball has a new entry in the lexicon of sports replay hand signals: A couple pats on the head.

video:video.video:title: Israel's parliament approves the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis
video:video.video:description: Israel’s parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis, a measure that has been harshly condemned by the international community and rights groups as discriminatory and inhumane.

video:video.video:title: Replay hand signals are everywhere in sports
video:video.video:description: Major League Baseball has a new entry in the lexicon of sports replay hand signals: A couple pats on the head.

video:video.video:title: Cuba prepares to receive first oil shipment this year
video:video.video:description: Cuba prepared Monday to receive a sanctioned Russian tanker carrying roughly 730,000 barrels of oil, the first such fuel delivery this year to the island that has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

video:video.video:title: A 'World Cup' for immigrant girls uses the joy of sport to counter ICE fears
video:video.video:description: A community advocate and immigrant from Bhutan created the event to help provide a sense of joy and unity amid federal immigration enforcement operations that have affected players' families.

video:video.video:title: TSA workers get paid and some lines improve — but ICE isn’t leaving yet
video:video.video:description: TSA workers are getting paid again and some lines are improving — but ICE officers are still at airports.

video:video.video:title: US senators visit Taiwan to reinforce security and economic ties
video:video.video:description: A bipartisan group of four U.S. senators arrived in Taiwan early Monday for a two-day visit aimed at strengthening alliances in the Indo-Pacific and reaffirming Washington’s support for the island.

video:video.video:title: Israel's parliament passes budget in marathon overnight session
video:video.video:description: Israel's parliament passes budget in marathon overnight session

video:video.video:title: Central African Republic's President Faustin-Archange Touadéra sworn in for third term
video:video.video:description: The president of the Central African Republic, Faustin-Archange Touadéra, was sworn in for a third term on Monday three months after a disputed general election.

video:video.video:title: Israel's parliament approves the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis
video:video.video:description: Israel’s parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis, a measure that has been harshly condemned by the international community and rights groups as discriminatory and inhumane.

video:video.video:title: Jonathan Tropper: Ryan Gosling brings 'charisma' to Star Wars
video:video.video:description: Ryan Gosling leads the latest film in the "Star Wars" franchise, which is scheduled to hit theaters in 2027. (March 30)

video:video.video:title: Fire at oil refinery in Israel's Haifa for second time during Iran war
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Monday at an oil refinery in the northern Israeli city of Haifa for the second time during the monthlong war with Iran, according to footage from the scene.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya's something old, new, borrowed and....
video:video.video:description: Is Zendaya embracing her bridal era during "The Drama" press tour? On theme with the movie's wedding storyline, the actor seems to be bringing the tradition of wearing something old, something new, something borrowed and something blue to red carpets worldwide.

video:video.video:title: UN says 3 peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon in 24 hours
video:video.video:description: The United Nations’ most powerful body will convene an emergency session Tuesday after officials announced that three peacekeepers in southern Lebanon had been killed in the last 24 hours.

video:video.video:title: Olivia Munn on Savannah Guthrie: 'She doesn't deserve this'
video:video.video:description: Munn has co-hosted the "Today" show and says she is heartbroken for Guthrie, whose mother remaining missing. (March 30)

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of military strikes in Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Central Command on Monday posted on the social media platform X video of military strikes in Iran.

video:video.video:title: The revival efforts for traditional Punjabi Phulkari embroidery
video:video.video:description: The traditional “flower work” embroidery from India’s northern Punjab region is being reimagined by artisans to preserve its original techniques.

video:video.video:title: Auschwitz survivor Ginette Kolinka reflects on Nazi horrors at 101
video:video.video:description: After surviving Auschwitz-Birkenau, Ginette Kolinka has become a mighty warrior against antisemitism in France, seeing purpose in sharing her firsthand insight of murderous hatred and inhumanity.

video:video.video:title: U.S. Central Command releases video of military strikes in Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Central Command on Monday posted on the social media platform X video of military strikes in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Business brains turn waste food into sought after oil and fuel in Kenya
video:video.video:description: Kenya has given rise to a new business community called "wastepreneurs" - entrepreneurs who take waste food and turn it into essential products such as fuel, fertilizer and cooking oil.

video:video.video:title: 'Decisive day' for humpback whale stranded off German coast amid fading hopes it can regain strength
video:video.video:description: A humpback whale stranded off Germany's north coast faces a critical moment in its hopes for survival, experts said Monday.

video:video.video:title: The Supreme Court will decide whether Trump’s birthright citizenship order violates the Constitution
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments on Wednesday over the constitutionality of President Donald Trump’s order on birthright citizenship.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Apollo to Artemis: A look at the legacy of NASA’s moon missions and what’s next
video:video.video:description: NASA is going back to the moon more than a half-century after Apollo. NASA’s Artemis program is a follow-up to the Apollo moonshots of the late 1960s and early 1970s.

video:video.video:title: Cuba prepares to receive first oil shipment this year after ease in US blockade
video:video.video:description: Cuba prepared on Monday to receive a sanctioned Russian tanker carrying roughly 730,000 barrels of oil, marking the island’s first such delivery this year.

video:video.video:title: The Supreme Court to hear arguments this week over Trump’s birthright citizenship order
video:video.video:description: Justices could decide this summer the constitutionality of Trump’s order. The 14th Amendment and federal law are widely understood to make citizens of everyone born in the country, with narrow exceptions.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Jews worldwide celebrate Passover
video:video.video:description: Passover is a major Jewish holiday, celebrated over the course of a week each year in springtime. It commemorates the exodus of ancient Israelites from slavery in Egypt, as recounted in the Bible.

video:video.video:title: Sky turned red ahead of tropical cyclone in Western Australia
video:video.video:description: The sky turned blood red in Western Australia’s Shark Bay ahead of the arrival of Tropical Cyclone Narelle on Friday.

video:video.video:title: 'We are still living in a war': Palestinian mourns brother among three men killed by Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Mourners prayed outside the Shifa Hospital in northern Gaza on Monday for three members of Hamas' military wing who were killed in an Israeli airstrike.

video:video.video:title: The giant Easter egg that weighs 55kg
video:video.video:description: British chocolate maker Cadbury has unveiled a giant 55 kilogram (121.25 pounds) egg ahead of Easter.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Trump says US could move to seize control of Iran's Kharg oil export hub
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested American troops could seize Iran's Kharg Island oil export hub, even as he added in comments published Monday that a deal with Tehran to end the war “could be made fairly quickly.”

video:video.video:title: Apollo to Artemis: A look at the legacy of NASA’s moon missions and what’s next
video:video.video:description: NASA is going back to the moon more than a half-century after Apollo. The Artemis mission with four astronauts on board won't land on the moon this time or even orbit it.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian inmates reduce sentences through reading
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of detainees across Brazil — including former President Jair Bolsonaro — signed up for a sentence reduction program that encourages inmates to immerse themselves in literary works in exchange for reducing their sentences by up to 48 days per year.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike targets apartment building in Beirut’s southern suburbs
video:video.video:description: Smoke billowed Monday from an apartment targeted by an Israeli airstrike in the southern suburbs of Beirut.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke cloud rises from fire at factory hit by missile fragment in southern Israel
video:video.video:description: A missile fragment that fell on a factory in southern Israel set a pesticide tank on fire sending black plumes of smoke and billowing flames high into the air.

video:video.video:title: Buses and trucks conduct slow-moving protest against rising fuel prices in Paris
video:video.video:description: Dozens of buses and trucks conducted a slow-moving protest on Paris' Boulevard Périphérique on Monday against the rise in fuel prices due to the war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Taiwan fashion designer stuns audience with avant-garde costumes
video:video.video:description: A fashion designer has taken inspiration from historic museums for his latest collection. On the catwalk at Taipei Fashion Week, Justin Chou's clothes include wigs, jackets and crowns drawn from paintings of the European nobles of the past.

video:video.video:title: US stocks fall in another shaky day as oil prices keep climbing
video:video.video:description: After jumping to an initial gain of 0.9%, the S&P 500 quickly erased nearly all of it before seesawing lower.

video:video.video:title: Iraqi military aircraft destroyed in missile attack on Baghdad air base
video:video.video:description: A rocket attack targeted an Iraqi air base west of Baghdad's airport destroyed a military aircraft, a spokesperson for the country's defense ministry said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Fed Chair Powell talks about supply shocks from Iran war in appearance at Harvard
video:video.video:description: Fed Chair Jerome Powell discussed energy price spikes caused by the Iran war and other issues during a talk Monday at Harvard University

video:video.video:title: Former Pakistani ambassador analyzes prospects for ending Iran war as his country seeks to mediate
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s former ambassador to Iran said prospects for renewed U.S.-Iran dialogue may depend on an initial ceasefire and mutual willingness to ease hostilities, while warning that continued conflict could have far-reaching global consequences.

video:video.video:title: Mexico weighs oil cooperation with Cuba as Trump signals openness to Russian shipment
video:video.video:description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday her government is exploring both humanitarian aid and potential commercial fuel arrangements with Cuba, as shifting U.S. signals and a pending Russian oil delivery draw renewed attention to the island’s energy crisis.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 31
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday March 31st: Likely U.S. strike in city that houses Iranian nuclear facility; UN peacekeepers and Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon; FBI says Michigan synagogue attacker inspired by Hezbollah; TSA officers receive backpay.

video:video.video:title: Ryan Gosling enters the 'Star Wars' universe
video:video.video:description: "Star Wars: Starfighter" writer Jonathan Tropper reveals what he thinks the movie's star Ryan Gosling will bring to the sci-fi franchise.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian toddlers evacuated in 2023 as premature babies return to families in a transformed Gaza
video:video.video:description: A group of toddlers evacuated as premature infants just a month into the Israel-Hamas war returned Monday to families overjoyed yet worried about their futures in a transformed Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Olivia Munn on 'heartbreaking' Guthrie situation
video:video.video:description: Actor Olivia Munn - who has co-hosted the "Today" show - says she is heartbroken for presenter Savannah Guthrie, whose mother has been missing for two months.

video:video.video:title: AP reports from the scene of a strike in central Israel
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded as civilians and rescue teams inspected an impact site in the central Israeli city of Bnei Brak, while people began moving quickly to find shelter ahead of an approaching missile attack.

video:video.video:title: Meet the rare Alborn Skink lizards facing extinction
video:video.video:description: Fewer than 30 Alborn skinks remain in their last known habitat in a wetland on New Zealand's South Island, and a rescue effort is underway to save them from extinction.

video:video.video:title: Asia gets its own Eurovision Song Contest
video:video.video:description: Music spectacle Eurovision is expanding with its first Asian edition, to be held in Bangkok, Thailand, later this year. (March 31)

video:video.video:title: Hegseth urges allies to ‘step up’ to open Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Secretary of Defense Hegseth says “there are countries around the world who ought be prepared to step up,” chastising allies for not doing more to clear the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: NASA is launching its first astronaut moonshot in 50 years. Here's what to know
video:video.video:description: Humans are going back around the moon for the first time since 1972. Four astronauts will hurtle several thousand miles beyond the moon, hang a U-turn and then come straight back during the nearly 10-day mission.

video:video.video:title: Rescue teams inspect damage after officials say Israeli city hit by cluster munition
video:video.video:description: Israeli officials said the central city of Bnei Brak was hit by a cluster munition on Tuesday as US President Donald Trump and Iranian officials issued contradictory statements about negotiations to end their war.

video:video.video:title: Jon Hamm returns with 'Your Friends & Neighbors'
video:video.video:description: Stars Jon Hamm, Amanda Peet, and Olivia Munn discuss what audiences can expect from the second season of "Your Friends & Neighbors" at the show’s New York premiere.

video:video.video:title: NASA is launching its first astronaut moonshot in 50 years. Here's what to know
video:video.video:description: It's humanity's first flight to the moon since 1972. Four astronauts will hurtle several thousand miles beyond the moon, hang a U-turn and then come straight back during the nearly 10-day mission.

video:video.video:title: US strikes on Iran's Isfahan send massive fireball into night sky
video:video.video:description: Witnesses captured huge blasts and fires early Tuesday over Isfahan, home to a major Iranian air base and other military sites, as well as one of Iran’s nuclear sites.

video:video.video:title: Trump threatens to destroy Iran’s civilian infrastructure if a deal is not reached ‘shortly’
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Monday threatened widespread destruction of Iran’s energy resources and other vital infrastructure, including desalination plants, if a deal to end the war is not reached “shortly.”

video:video.video:title: Iran Red Crescent video shows search for survivors in aftermath of attacks
video:video.video:description: Debris was scattered along the streets in Tehran and Zanjan as rescue workers searched through the rubble to find survivors after strikes hit the cities.

video:video.video:title: Brandy hopes her journey teaches authenticity
video:video.video:description: Here's what Brandy hopes her journey teaches young women. She was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Bucha remembers the hundreds killed by Russian troops in fourth anniversary memorial ceremony
video:video.video:description: Residents in Bucha on Monday commemorated those killed by Russia's invading forces as they marked the fourth anniversary of atrocities committed in the town near Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: Gas prices in the US surge past $4 a gallon, ‘uncomfortable level’ for drivers, analyst says
video:video.video:description: U.S. gas prices jump past an average of $4 a gallon for the first time since 2022, reaching an “uncomfortable level” for motorists, an analyst says.

video:video.video:title: Celebs support as Brandy gets star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Brandy was joined at her Hollywood Walk of Fame ceremony Monday by Babyface, Issa Rae, Monica, Kehlani and "Moesha" co-stars Shar Jackson and Marcus T. Paulk.

video:video.video:title: How to fill your home with quality furniture that doesn't break the bank
video:video.video:description: Furnishing a home doesn’t have to mean buying everything new. Fast furniture is often made with cheaper materials that aren’t built to last — and don’t break down in landfills.

video:video.video:title: Trump tells allies to 'go get your own oil' via social media post with Strait of Hormuz still closed
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump lashed out at allies over their unwillingness to help the US reopen the critical passageway in a recent social media post.

video:video.video:title: Russian oil tanker arrives in Cuba, the first in three months as island struggles under US blockade
video:video.video:description: Russian vessel Anatoly Kolodkin docked Tuesday at the Cuban port of Matanzas laden with 730,000 barrels of oil, marking the first time in three months that an oil tanker reached the island.

video:video.video:title: From Macintosh to iPhone: 50 years of Apple gadgets celebrated in new museum
video:video.video:description: The innovations of Apple Inc. are on show at a new museum in the Netherlands. It charts the company's journey its first experimental models to the world-changing iPhone.

video:video.video:title: Theo James goes undercover as a builder
video:video.video:description: Between takes on his latest movie "Fuze," actor Theo James stayed in character and convinced local builders that he was a tradesman too.

video:video.video:title: Ohio jury can't reach verdict in corruption trial of 2 ex-FirstEnergy executives
video:video.video:description: Jurors say they can’t reach a verdict in the trial of two fired FirstEnergy Corp. executives in a $60 million bribery scheme in Ohio.

video:video.video:title: Israeli rocket strikes building in Beirut
video:video.video:description: A little over an hour after sending an evacuation notice to a building in a southern suburb of Beirut, Israeli warplanes struck the building, destroying it.

video:video.video:title: Penguin steals items from another nest as nest building season begins at Chicago aquarium
video:video.video:description: It’s officially penguin nesting season at Shedd Aquarium, giving visitors a rare, close-up look at one of the busiest — and most charming — times of year.

video:video.video:title: Israeli civil rights group sets out its appeal against 'barbaric' death penalty law
video:video.video:description: The Association of Civil Rights in Israel said it had already petitioned the country’s highest court to challenge a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis.

video:video.video:title: Israeli rocket strikes building in Beirut
video:video.video:description: A little over an hour after sending an evacuation notice to a building in a southern suburb of Beirut, Israeli warplanes struck the building, destroying it.

video:video.video:title: How one family in Havana grapples with daily life as economic crisis deepens
video:video.video:description: As the U.S continues to blockade the island of Cuba families already struggling are being pushed to the brink (APVideo shot by: Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán)

video:video.video:title: Palestinians protest across West Bank against Israeli death penalty law
video:video.video:description: Palestinians young and old held sit-ins and marches in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Tuesday after Israel's parliament passed a measure establishing the death penalty by hanging for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis.

video:video.video:title: Shia mosque in Iran sustains heavy damage in deadly US-Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: A US-Israeli airstrike hit a Shia mosque in the northwestern city of Zanjan early on Tuesday, according to Iranian state media.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s name to be added to an airport and a new presidential library
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump’s name will soon be added to a Florida airport and a new presidential library in Miami. Here's what to know.

video:video.video:title: Gargoyle installation marks end of major construction on 'America's Notre Dame'
video:video.video:description: Last gargoyle installation at Kentucky cathedral modeled after Notre Dame caps two year restoration project.

video:video.video:title: Trump officials exempt oil and gas drilling in Gulf of Mexico from endangered species rules
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration has won an exemption for oil and gas drilling in the Gulf of Mexico from the Endangered Species Act on the basis of national security.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Jerusalem as Israel approves death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murder
video:video.video:description: Israeli police used a water cannon on Tuesday to disperse dozens of protesters in Jerusalem after Israel's parliament approved the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis.

video:video.video:title: Babyface shares what he and Whitney Houston said about Brandy
video:video.video:description: Babyface shares what he and Whitney Houston said about Brandy while working on the 1995 "Waiting to Exhale" soundtrack.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s name to be added to an airport and a new presidential library
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump’s name will soon be added to a Florida airport and a new presidential library in Miami. Here's what to know.

video:video.video:title: Alessia Cara says she feels less pressure in the music industry
video:video.video:description: "It's all one big playing field." Alessia Cara on how the music industry has changed since she won the best new artist Grammy Award in 2018.

video:video.video:title: Emergency workers search for victims as Israeli airstrikes on Beirut continue
video:video.video:description: Israeli airstrikes on the Lebanese capital continued through the evening into early Wednesday. The Jnah neighborhood of Beirut sustained a hit, and Associated Press journalists saw at least one body bag being removed from the scene.

video:video.video:title: Sting looks to Mick Jagger as inspiration for performing into old age
video:video.video:description: Sting says he looks to Mick Jagger as inspiration for longevity in performing arts. Sting's musical "The Last Ship" comes to the Metropolitan Opera June 9-14.

video:video.video:title: Matanzas residents hold little hope that life will improve in Cuba despite oil tanker arrival
video:video.video:description: Residents of Cuba's Matanzas, where the Russian oil tanker docked Tuesday morning, hold little hope life will improve despite its arrival.
video:video.video:tag: Mery Ramos Barceló Mery Ramos Barceló

video:video.video:title: Andrew McCarthy explains how he started writing
video:video.video:description: Andrew McCarthy reveals the 'ah ha' moment that inspired him to write.

video:video.video:title: Trump signs order for creation of national voter list in a move sure to face legal challenges
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says “the cheating on mail-in voting is legendary. It's horrible what's going on,” repeating his false allegations about mail ballots as he signed the executive order.

video:video.video:title: Weezer still has the 'fire to create new music'
video:video.video:description: Weezer members Rivers Cuomo and Brian Bell reflect on the band's biggest hits after announcing their 2026 tour, "Weezer: The Gathering."

video:video.video:title: Trump attends 'Chicago' musical opening night at the Kennedy Center
video:video.video:description: The outing could be one of the president's last visits at the Kennedy Center before it closes for a two-year renovation.

video:video.video:title: King Charles to make a state visit to the US despite calls to cancel due to the Iran war
video:video.video:description: King Charles III will undertake a state visit to the United States in late April to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American Independence and to mark the close ties that exist between the two nations.

video:video.video:title: Trump: US could leave Iran in 2 to 3 weeks, securing Strait of Hormuz is 'not for us'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday the U.S. will not "have anything to do with” securing the Strait of Hormuz and predicted attacks on Iran could end within 2 to 3 weeks.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu says Iran no longer threat to Israel's existence, details security buffer zones
video:video.video:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday said that although Iran still had launch capability to threaten Israel, it could no longer threaten its existence.

video:video.video:title: Stunning traditional Easter eggs made by women in Slovenia
video:video.video:description: The intricate designs are scraped with a knife on eggs that have been dyed reddish brown with onion skins by women in south-eastern Slovenia. (AP video by Tina Smole

video:video.video:title: Large sand storm sweeps across Sahara
video:video.video:description: A large sand storm was seen sweeping across the Sahara in western Algeria on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Golden toilet installed on the National Mall, a satire of Trump's White House renovations
video:video.video:description: This latest sculpture, designed by Secret Handshake, a resistance art group that has installed several pieces of artwork on the Mall, protests the Trump administration.

video:video.video:title: How one family in Havana grapples with daily life as economic crisis
video:video.video:description: Persistent blackouts, cuts to the state-run food ration system, and severe shortages of both water and medicine have transformed daily life into an ordeal for families like Riviaux, her husband Cristóbal Estrada and their two daughters.

video:video.video:title: Judge blocks Trump order to end funding for National Public Radio and Public Broadcasting Service
video:video.video:description: A federal judge has agreed to permanently block the Trump administration from implementing a presidential directive to end federal funding for National Public Radio and the Public Broadcasting Service.

video:video.video:title: S&P 500 surges 2.9%, biggest gain since last spring as hopes build for an end to Iran war
video:video.video:description: U.S. stocks surged to their best day since last spring, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average soared more than 1,100 points as doubt swings back to hope on Wall Street for a possible end to the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth puts allies on notice over Iran: Might want to learn 'how to fight for yourself'
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warned US allies Tuesday to do more to support the war with Iran or "start learning how to fight" for themselves, echoing President Donald Trump’s criticism that some partners have not done enough to support US efforts.

video:video.video:title: Big ears, big energy: Threatened painted dog pups make debut at Chicago area zoo
video:video.video:description: Big ears, big energy: Threatened painted dog pups make debut at Chicago area zoo

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 31
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, March 31st: The Supreme Court ruled against a law banning “conversion therapy”; Israel uses water cannon on people protesting new law; Colombian army rescues six siblings from jungle; Oil tanker arrives in Cuba

video:video.video:title: Gas prices eclipse $4 a gallon in the US, the highest since 2022, as Iran war impacts fuel costs
video:video.video:description: U.S. gas prices jumped past an average of $4 a gallon for the first time since 2022 on Tuesday, as the Iran war pushes fuel prices to soar worldwide.

video:video.video:title: Drivers in the US react as gas prices eclipse $4 a gallon
video:video.video:description: Drivers expressed frustration with President Donald Trump, blaming his decision to engage in a war with Iran as gas prices climbed above $4 a gallon for the first time since 2022.

video:video.video:title: Judge orders Trump administration to halt White House ballroom construction unless Congress OKs it
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration was ordered to suspend its construction of a $400 million ballroom where it demolished the East Wing of the White House, barring work from proceeding without congressional approval.

video:video.video:title: Australia accuses major tech firms of 'failing to obey the laws' over online child account ban
video:video.video:description: Australia is considering bringing court action against Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube after alleging they are not doing enough to keep Australian children younger than 16 off their platforms.

video:video.video:title: EU warns oil and gas prices won't immediately return to normal — even if Iran war ends
video:video.video:description: Skyrocketing oil and gas prices in Europe as a result of the ongoing Iran war won't return to normal levels any time soon, even if peace is declared tomorrow, the European Union's energy commissioner warned Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Sadie Sink and Noah Jupe say 'Romeo and Juliet' is a team effort
video:video.video:description: Sadie Sink and Noah Jupe say that starring as the famous star-crossed lovers has been much easier because of their shared support for each other.

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti hopes 'The Testaments' will inspire Gen-Z to feel empowered and 'enact change'
video:video.video:description: Chase Infiniti stars in the highly anticipated "Handmaid's Tale" spin-off "The Testaments" and hopes the new series will inspire Gen Z to create change in their own communities.

video:video.video:title: Tensions remain high at Israeli-Lebanese border as Israel outlines plans for invasion
video:video.video:description: Stefanie Black lives in the northern Israeli Kibbutz of Amir and said that "people feel unsafe here".

video:video.video:title: Column of smoke rises skywards in Tehran following latest Israel-US strike
video:video.video:description: Explosions were heard and a column of smoke was seen rising in Tehran early on Wednesday following the latest Israel-U.S. strike on the Iranian capital.

video:video.video:title: Meet the crew behind NASA's latest mission to the moon
video:video.video:description: NASA is preparing for its first crewed mission towards the moon in more than fifty years. The Artemis II mission will not land on the lunar surface, but it's a critical step in testing deep space travel and the crew reflects a new generation of international and more diverse human spaceflight.

video:video.video:title: Good Friday a solemn day for Christians, with ancient prayers and fervent processions
video:video.video:description: Celebrated the Friday before Easter, Good Friday commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus. This year, it will fall on April 3 for Catholics and Protestants, and April 10 for Orthodox Christians.
